title: The bean that Spring couldn't find

mcq.question_md: Your main class with `@SpringBootApplication` lives in package `com.fledge.app`. You create `MessMenuService`, mark it `@Service`, and put it in package `com.fledge.utils`. At startup Spring fails with *"required a bean of type MessMenuService that could not be found"*. What's the most likely cause?
options: `@Service` only works on classes that implement an interface
The class sits outside `com.fledge.app`, so component scanning never visits it
You must also register the class in an XML configuration file
`@Service` classes need a no-argument constructor to be detected
mcq.explanation_md: `@SpringBootApplication` includes `@ComponentScan`, which searches the main class's package **and below** — `com.fledge.app` and its sub-packages. `com.fledge.utils` is a sibling, not a child, so the scan never sees the class and the annotation on it does nothing. Fix: move the class under the base package (usual) or widen the scan with `scanBasePackages`. The XML option tempts people who've seen old Spring tutorials — XML config exists but Boot apps don't need it. Interfaces and no-arg constructors are not requirements for detection.
